# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an ARM template for Azure Virtual Network with subnets for a three-tier application (web, app, database). Use basic VNet configuration with default settings for rapid deployment. Skip DDoS protection and advanced networking features to reduce costs in our development environment.
# Category: cloud_network_security

resources:
- name: vnet_resourcegroup
  type: Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks@2019-11-01
  properties:
    location: 'West Europe' # choose your region here, example - WestEurope or EastUS etc...  
    addressSpace: ['10.0.0.0/16']#example of an IP space for the VNet in CIDR notation (e.g., ‘10.0.0.0/24’)
- name: vnet_websubnet
  type: Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/subnets@2019-11-01   # specify the subresource and its version here (in this case, it's 'Microsoft Network virtual networks SubNets @2019 - 11 - 1')
  properties:
    addressPrefix: ['10.0.2.0/24']#example of a IP space for the Web tier in CIDR notation (e.g., ‘10.0.2.0/24’)   # example - '10.0.2.0/24'
- name: vnet_appsubnet
  type: Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/subnets@2019-11-01   
  properties:
    addressPrefix: ['10.0.3.0/24']#example of a IP space for the App tier in CIDR notation (e.g., '10.0.3.0/24')   # example -'10.0.3.0/24’
- name: vnet_dbsubnet 
  type: Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/subnets@2019-11-01   
  properties:
     addressPrefix: ['10.0.5.0/24']#example of a IP space for the Database tier in CIDR notation (e.g., '10.0.5.0/24')   # example -'10.0.5.0/24’
parameters: 
- name: location_param   
  type: string    
  defaultValue: West Europe      # choose your region here, for instance East US or North Central USA etc... (default value is set as a placeholder)
